parameters.jsCode:
const res = Array.isArray($json.organic_results) ? $json.organic_results : [];
return [{ json: { linkedin: res.filter(r => r.link && r.link.includes('linkedin.com')).slice(0,5).map(r => ({ title: r.title, url: r.link, snippet: r.snippet })) } }];

const res = Array.isArray($json.organic_results) ? $json.organic_results : [];
return [{ json: { social: res.slice(0,5).map(r => ({ title: r.title, url: r.link, snippet: r.snippet })) } }];

parameters.jsonBody:
={{ { "model": "llama-3.3-70b-versatile", "messages": [ { "role": "system", "content": "You are a Red Team OSINT specialist. Analyze the gathered intelligence data. Focus strictly on actionable intelligence, attack surface mapping, discovered personnel/emails, and potential social engineering vectors.

CRITICAL RULES:
1) DIGITAL FOOTPRINT / LINKEDIN: You MUST explicitly output the raw, clickable URLs for ALL discovered LinkedIn profiles and social media links exactly as they appear in the data. Do NOT summarize them away.
2) EMAIL INTELLIGENCE: Do NOT guess or generate emails! The OSINT data contains an 'emails' array with ALL discovered organizational emails. You MUST list EVERY single entry in that array - email address, name (if present), position and department. Infer the target from the email prefix if no name is attached. List the rest as Secondary Targets.
3) EMAIL PATTERN: State the discovered email pattern so readers can construct additional target addresses.

Include: 1) Executive Summary, 2) Target Footprint & LinkedIn Links (RAW URLS), 3) Discovered Organizational Emails - ALL ENTRIES, 4) Suggested Attack Vectors." }, { "role": "user", "content": "Target: " + $node["Parse Target Input"].json.targetName + ($node["Parse Target Input"].json.company ? " from " + $node["Parse Target Input"].json.company : "") + "
OSINT Data:
" + JSON.stringify($json) } ] } }}
